author: HWY1dot0
description: Find every note created on a given day from one calendar view, no matter which folder it lives in. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: HWY1dot0
manifest.description: See every note from a given day in one calendar view — daily notes, meeting notes, logs — no matter which folder they live in.
manifest.name: Calendar Hub

name: Calendar Hub

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Review-fix release: identical features to 0.2.0, which did not clear Obsidian's automated plugin review. Declares `minAppVersion` 1.7.2 (required by the current `workspace.revealLeaf` API) and cleans up the flagged type assertions.

Everything below is what's new since 0.1.13:

The date-matching engine grew up, and notes got a second way to be browsed.

## New

- **List view** — browse every indexed note grouped by Year, Year › Quarter, Year › Month, or Year › Week, newest or oldest first, with note counts on group headers. The sidebar calendar pane flips to it in place (button at the top of the pane, themed like the file explorer's controls), or open it as its own tab with the **Open list view** command and drag it anywhere — including the main workspace for full-height browsing.
- **List filter lens** — the list carries its own folder filter, separate from the calendar's scan scope: focus on one area of the vault without touching the calendar.
- **Folder rules with exclusions** — folder filters accept `-` prefixed entries to exclude a subtree, and deeper rules win: `30 Macro, -30 Macro/Archive` scans 30 Macro except its Archive. Settings shows the vault's folders as a collapsible checkbox tree; unchecking a covered subfolder writes the exclusion for you.
- **File creation date fallback** (off by default) — map notes without any declared date to the day the file was created. Declared dates always win.
- **Format presets** — a dropdown of common filename date formats next to the text field; each pick appends to the list.

## Changed

- **Frontmatter dates now take priority over filename dates**, with creation time last. Only notes where both a filename date and a frontmatter date exist and disagree are affected; frontmatter matching remains opt-in with user-configured fields.
- Frontmatter date fields are tried in the order you configure them — the first field with a valid date wins, so put the field you trust most first.
- The settings tab is regrouped to mirror the matching pipeline, and the scan scope is configured in Settings — the sidebar no longer carries a global scope switch.

## Fixed

- Editing a frontmatter date now moves the note on the calendar within a second. Previously the reindex raced Obsidian's metadata cache and the calendar stayed stale until an unrelated file event.
- Filename formats with a `Z`/`ZZ` offset token now match literal-Z timestamps such as `20260709T143025Z`, and offset timestamps bucket to the calendar day written in the name instead of converting to the machine's timezone — the same vault now buckets identically on machines in different timezones.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
## Weekly notes overhaul

**Self-contained weekly notes.** Calendar Hub no longer reads weekly-note settings from the legacy Calendar plugin. The weekly note format, folder, and template you configure in Calendar Hub are now actually used (no more silent fallback to `gggg-[W]ww`).

**Fixes**
- Newly created weekly notes now show on the calendar immediately. Previously, with "Detect daily notes in all folders" enabled, a new weekly note only appeared after a reload. Delete/rename now refresh the weekly index too.

**New — weekly notes aggregate like daily notes**
- Week numbers show a note-count dot, and selecting a week lists every note for that week below the calendar (same panel as days).
- Notes are matched by a week-format filename or a frontmatter week field, across folders — multiple notes per week.
- New settings under *Weekly Note Settings*: detect weekly notes in all folders, folders to scan, extra filename formats, and a frontmatter fallback with configurable fields.

Install/update via Community Plugins, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
Maintenance release addressing Obsidian automated review.

- Fix build verification: `npm run build` (rollup) now completes in a clean environment; linting/type-checking moved out of the build step.
- Bump svelte 3.35.0 → 3.59.2 and moment 2.29.4 to clear dependency advisories.

No user-facing behavior changes.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Initial Calendar Hub release: find every note created on a given day from one calendar view, with folder filters, filename date matching, frontmatter fallback, and note-count dots.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
# Multi Note Calendar beta

This is a public beta fork of Liam Cain's Calendar plugin for Obsidian.

It is intended for users who keep multiple date-based notes per day across different folders.

## Added in this beta

- Multiple notes per date.
- A selected-day note list below the calendar.
- Vault-wide filename matching.
- Extra filename date formats such as `YYYYMMDD`.
- Frontmatter date fallback.
- Folder filters from both settings and the Calendar sidebar.
- Solid dots represent note count instead of word count.

## Attribution

This fork is based on the original Calendar plugin by Liam Cain:

https://github.com/liamcain/obsidian-calendar-plugin

The original project is MIT licensed, and the original copyright notice remains in place.

## Upstream status

An upstream proposal/PR has been opened. This fork is published under a separate name so users who need this workflow can test it without replacing the official Calendar plugin.

## Beta warning

This fork changes Calendar's date model from one note per date to multiple notes per date. Please test it on a vault you can recover from backup.
releases.name: 0.1.0-beta.1
